summary: Pada hari pemilihan pendahuluan di New York, para sosialis demokrat yang didukung oleh Walikota Zohran Mamdani menantang tiga anggota DPR incumbent dari Partai Demokrat, menguji pengaruh politiknya. Mahkamah Agung akan segera memutuskan kasus penting yang mungkin mendefinisikan kekuasaan presiden. Badan intelijen mulai memecat orang-orang yang disebut 'deep state'. Tucker Carlson mengumumkan tidak lagi mendukung Partai Republik. Para tamu acara membahas kebangkitan sosialis demokrat, perpecahan internal partai, dan pengaruh AIPAC.

vocabulary.en: the day when primary elections are held
vocabulary.excerpt: it's primary day in New York
vocabulary.example_cn: Hari ini adalah Hari Pemilihan Pendahuluan di New York.

vocabulary.en: a person who advocates for democratic socialism, combining socialist economic policies with democratic political systems
vocabulary.excerpt: Can more Democratic socialists ride Mayor Mamdani's wave?
vocabulary.example_cn: Bisakah lebih banyak Sosialis Demokrat menang dengan mengikuti gelombang Wali Kota Mamdani?

vocabulary.en: to take advantage of a trend or momentum
vocabulary.excerpt: hoping to ride the wave of Democratic socialists
vocabulary.example_cn: 希望乘着民主社会主义者的浪潮。

vocabulary.en: the current holder of a political office
vocabulary.excerpt: Two are going up against Democratic incumbents
vocabulary.example_cn: 两人正挑战民主党现任议员。

vocabulary.en: the period during which someone holds a position
vocabulary.excerpt: tested just six months into his tenure
vocabulary.example_cn: 在他上任仅六个月时受到考验。

vocabulary.en: a list of candidates for election
vocabulary.excerpt: This slate here today is our answer.
vocabulary.example_cn: 今天这份候选人名单就是我们的回答。

vocabulary.en: favoring social reform and progressive policies
vocabulary.excerpt: I'm a longtime progressive
vocabulary.example_cn: 我是一名长期的进步派。

vocabulary.en: the political system dominated by established parties and interests
vocabulary.excerpt: felt deeply abandoned by the establishment politics
vocabulary.example_cn: Sangat merasa ditinggalkan oleh politik mapan.

vocabulary.en: factors that help progress or success
vocabulary.excerpt: he is trying to have, like, tailwinds
vocabulary.example_cn: Dia mencoba mendapatkan bantuan angin kencang.

vocabulary.en: to extend or project known information to unknown situations
vocabulary.excerpt: I don't know that we can extrapolate about the national Democratic Party
vocabulary.example_cn: Saya tidak tahu apakah kita bisa menyimpulkan situasi Demokrat nasional.

vocabulary.cn: Mengatasi kondisi yang tidak menguntungkan, bangkit dari keterpurukan
vocabulary.en: to succeed despite difficulties or low probability
vocabulary.excerpt: three Democratic socialists overcame the odds
vocabulary.example_cn: Tiga demokrat sosial berhasil bangkit dari keterpurukan.

vocabulary.cn: Daerah pemilihan medan perang, daerah pemilihan yang kompetitif
vocabulary.en: a electoral district where the outcome is uncertain and both parties compete heavily
vocabulary.excerpt: There's about 34 battleground districts
vocabulary.example_cn: Ada sekitar 34 daerah pemilihan medan perang.

vocabulary.cn: 边缘席位，胜负难分的席位
vocabulary.en: a parliamentary seat held by a small majority, easily lost
vocabulary.excerpt: in these marginal seats
vocabulary.example_cn: 在这些边缘席位中。

vocabulary.en: to separate or disassociate
vocabulary.excerpt: You're trying to divorce it here.
vocabulary.example_cn: 你试图在这里把它分开。

vocabulary.cn: 创造性紧张，建设性冲突
vocabulary.en: a productive conflict that leads to positive change
vocabulary.excerpt: Dr. King talked about creative tension being a catalyst for social justice
vocabulary.example_cn: 马丁·路德·金博士谈到创造性紧张是社会正义的催化剂。

vocabulary.cn: 扩大阵营，包容更多群体
vocabulary.en: to make a political party or movement more inclusive
vocabulary.excerpt: we are broadening the tent of the Democratic Party
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们正在扩大民主党的阵营。

vocabulary.en: opposed to the established social, political, or economic system
vocabulary.excerpt: it's really anti-establishment
vocabulary.example_cn: Ini sebenarnya anti-establishment.

vocabulary.en: the existing state of affairs
vocabulary.excerpt: running against the status quo
vocabulary.example_cn: Menentang status quo.

vocabulary.en: general election
vocabulary.excerpt: can they win in a general?
vocabulary.example_cn: Bisakah mereka memenangkan pemilihan umum?

vocabulary.en: a type of independent political action committee that can raise unlimited funds
vocabulary.excerpt: super PACs at large who are spending millions of dollars
vocabulary.example_cn: Super PAC menghabiskan jutaan dolar.

vocabulary.en: to cover completely
vocabulary.excerpt: deceptive and misleading ads that are blanketing airwaves
vocabulary.example_cn: Iklan yang menipu dan menyesatkan yang membanjiri.

vocabulary.cn: 令人恐惧的人或事物，妖怪
vocabulary.en: a person or thing that is widely feared or blamed
vocabulary.excerpt: AIPAC has become a real boogeyman in races
vocabulary.example_cn: AIPAC已成为竞选中的真正妖怪。

vocabulary.en: a serious disagreement or split
vocabulary.excerpt: created a political rift
vocabulary.example_cn: 造成了政治裂痕。

vocabulary.excerpt: exacerbated by October 7th
vocabulary.example_cn: 因10月7日事件而加剧。

sentences.en: And imagine opening your door and finding two million reasons to stay inside.
sentences.cn: 想象一下，打开门发现有两百万个理由让你待在屋里。
sentences.structure: 祈使句 + 并列动名词短语作宾语
sentences.analysis: 'two million reasons' 是比喻，指两百万只蜜蜂；'reasons to stay inside' 是名词短语，不定式作定语。

sentences.en: Video from Queen Bee Supply shows the chaos workers faced to capture the swarm.
sentences.cn: Queen Bee Supply 的视频展示了工人在捕捉蜂群时面临的混乱场面。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 省略关系代词的定语从句 'workers faced' + 不定式短语 'to capture the swarm'
sentences.analysis: 'the chaos workers faced' 是省略 that 的定语从句；'to capture the swarm' 表示目的。

sentences.en: Can more Democratic socialists ride Mayor Mamdani's wave?
sentences.cn: 更多的民主社会主义者能否乘着曼达尼市长的浪潮前进？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句，主语 'more Democratic socialists'，谓语 'ride'，宾语 'Mayor Mamdani's wave'
sentences.analysis: 'ride the wave' 是习语，意为'乘势而上'；'Mayor Mamdani's wave' 指曼达尼带来的政治势头。

sentences.en: Will justices go against the president in cases that could help define his power?
sentences.cn: 法官们会在可能界定总统权力的案件中反对总统吗？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句 + 定语从句 'that could help define his power' 修饰 'cases'
sentences.analysis: 'go against' 意为'反对'；'define his power' 指界定总统的权力范围。

sentences.en: And so-called deep-state firings have started at a top U.S. intelligence agency.
sentences.cn: 所谓的'深层政府'解雇行动已在美国一家顶级情报机构开始。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语 'so-called deep-state firings'，谓语 'have started'
sentences.analysis: 'so-called' 表示'所谓的'；'deep-state' 指'深层政府'，即被认为暗中操控政府的势力。

sentences.en: What else could the president's hand-picked temporary director do?
sentences.cn: 总统亲自挑选的临时主管还能做什么？
sentences.structure: 特殊疑问句，主语 'the president's hand-picked temporary director'，谓语 'could do'
sentences.analysis: 'hand-picked' 意为'精心挑选的'；'temporary director' 指临时主管。

sentences.en: He's backed candidates in three House races.
sentences.cn: 他支持了三个众议院席位的候选人。
sentences.structure: 现在完成时简单句，主语 'He'，谓语 'has backed'，宾语 'candidates'，状语 'in three House races'
sentences.analysis: 'back' 作动词意为'支持'；'House races' 指众议院选举。

sentences.en: Two are going up against Democratic incumbents, and they are hoping to ride the wave of Democratic socialists, who have been winning big races across the country.
sentences.cn: 两人正与民主党现任议员竞争，他们希望乘着民主社会主义者的浪潮前进，这些人在全国范围内赢得了重大选举。
sentences.structure: 并列句 + 非限制性定语从句 'who have been winning...' 修饰 'Democratic socialists'
sentences.analysis: 'go up against' 意为'对抗'；'ride the wave' 同上；'incumbents' 指现任者。

sentences.en: I am someone who has felt deeply abandoned by the establishment politics that far too often see my community as merely a statistic.
sentences.cn: 我是一个深感被建制政治抛弃的人，这种政治常常把我的社区仅仅视为一个统计数据。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 定语从句 'who has felt...' + 嵌套定语从句 'that far too often see...' 修饰 'establishment politics'
sentences.analysis: 'establishment politics' 指'建制派政治'；'see...as merely a statistic' 意为'仅仅视为一个数字'。

sentences.en: They're not handpicked by the party, yet somehow, they rise via the Internet and the power of their ideas.
sentences.cn: 他们并非由政党精心挑选，但不知何故，他们通过互联网和思想的力量崛起。
sentences.structure: 并列句，由 'yet' 连接，前后分句均为简单句
sentences.analysis: 'handpicked' 同上；'via' 意为'通过'；'the power of their ideas' 指思想的力量。

sentences.en: I don't know that we can extrapolate about the national Democratic Party from what happens in New York City.
sentences.cn: 我不知道我们能否从纽约市发生的事情推断出全国民主党的情况。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 宾语从句 'that we can extrapolate...' + 介词短语 'from what happens...'
sentences.analysis: 'extrapolate' 意为'推断'；'from what happens' 是介词 from 后接名词性从句。

sentences.en: Notice how the first thing out of his mouth in that answer is, 'If by Democratic socialist, you mean.'
sentences.cn: 注意，他在那个回答中脱口而出的第一句话是：'如果你说的民主社会主义者是指……'
sentences.structure: 祈使句 + 宾语从句 'how the first thing... is...' + 直接引语
sentences.analysis: 'the first thing out of his mouth' 是习语，意为'脱口而出的第一句话'；'If by... you mean' 是条件句的省略形式。

sentences.en: In political world, you don't beat incumbents.
sentences.cn: Dalam dunia politik, Anda tidak bisa mengalahkan petahana.
sentences.structure: Kalimat sederhana, subjek 'you', predikat 'don't beat', objek 'incumbents'
sentences.analysis: 'beat' berarti 'mengalahkan'; 'incumbents' merujuk pada pejabat petahana.

sentences.en: But that's much different than every other battleground district in America.
sentences.cn: Tapi ini sangat berbeda dengan setiap distrik medan pertempuran lainnya di Amerika.
sentences.structure: Kalimat sederhana, subjek 'that', kata kerja 'is', predikat 'much different than...'
sentences.analysis: 'much different than' adalah struktur perbandingan informal; 'battleground district' berarti 'distrik medan pertempuran', yaitu distrik dengan persaingan ketat.

sentences.en: The Democratic socialist message of being for the people, by the people.
sentences.cn: Pesan sosialis demokratis 'dari rakyat, oleh rakyat'.
sentences.structure: Frasa nomina, kata inti 'message', diikuti frasa preposisi 'of being for the people, by the people'
sentences.analysis: 'for the people, by the people' meniru pidato Gettysburg Lincoln 'of the people, by the people, for the people', di sini 'of the people' dihilangkan.

sentences.en: But in these marginal seats, they'll say everybody's a Democratic socialist.
sentences.cn: Tapi di distrik kursi marjinal ini, mereka akan mengatakan semua orang adalah sosialis demokratis.
sentences.structure: Kalimat sederhana, subjek 'they', predikat 'will say', klausa objek 'everybody's a Democratic socialist'
sentences.analysis: 'marginal seats' berarti 'kursi marjinal', yaitu distrik yang sulit diprediksi pemenangnya.

sentences.en: Dr. King talked about creative tension being a catalyst for social justice.
sentences.cn: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. berbicara tentang ketegangan kreatif sebagai katalis keadilan sosial.
sentences.structure: Kalimat sederhana, subjek 'Dr. King', predikat 'talked about', objek 'creative tension being a catalyst...'
sentences.analysis: 'creative tension' adalah konsep Dr. King, berarti ketegangan konstruktif; 'catalyst' berarti 'katalis'.

sentences.en: And clearly, given D.S. -- DSA and quote, unquote 'progressive Democrats,' we are moving forward, and we are broadening the tent of the Democratic Party.
sentences.cn: Jelas, mengingat D.S. -- DSA dan yang disebut 'Demokrat progresif', kita bergerak maju, kita memperluas tenda Partai Demokrat.
sentences.structure: Kalimat majemuk, diawali frasa preposisi 'given...' sebagai keterangan, diikuti dua klausa setara
sentences.analysis: 'given' berarti 'mengingat'; 'quote, unquote' berarti 'yang disebut'; 'broaden the tent' adalah metafora, berarti 'memperluas inklusivitas'.

sentences.en: I think this whole movement is two things: one, it's the Tea Party of the Republicans back in the day, or maybe even MAGA in some way.
sentences.cn: Saya pikir seluruh gerakan memiliki dua sisi: satu seperti gerakan Tea Party Partai Republik dulu, atau bahkan seperti MAGA sampai batas tertentu.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama + klausa objek + penjelasan setara setelah titik dua
sentences.analysis: 'Tea Party' mengacu pada gerakan Tea Party; 'MAGA' adalah singkatan dari 'Make America Great Again'; 'back in the day' berarti 'dulu'.

sentences.en: But what's really going to show me for the mayor, because I think that these -- he has a really good chance of winning these three races today.
sentences.cn: Tapi yang benar-benar akan membuktikan walikota bagi saya adalah, karena saya pikir dia memiliki peluang besar hari ini untuk memenangkan ketiga pemilihan ini.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama 'what's really going to show me for the mayor' + klausa keterangan sebab 'because...' + klausa objek 'that he has...'
sentences.analysis: 'show me for the mayor' berarti 'membuktikan walikota (kemampuannya)'; 'have a good chance of doing' berarti 'memiliki peluang besar untuk melakukan'.

topics.title: Pemilihan Pendahuluan New York dan Kebangkitan Sosialis Demokrat
topics.content: Pada hari pemilihan pendahuluan New York, 23 Juni 2026, para sosialis demokrat (Democratic socialists) yang didukung oleh Walikota Zohran Mamdani menantang tiga anggota Kongres Demokrat petahana. Mamdani terpilih pada tahun 2025, dan timnya berusaha meniru keberhasilan sosialis demokrat di seluruh negeri, seperti di Washington D.C., Seattle, dan tempat lain. Langkah ini menguji pengaruh politik Mamdani dan mencerminkan ketegangan antara faksi progresif dan mapan di dalam Partai Demokrat.
topics.keywords: primary day · Democratic socialist · incumbent · slate · progressive

topics.title: Putusan Mahkamah Agung dan Kekuasaan Presiden
topics.content: Acara tersebut menyebutkan bahwa Mahkamah Agung akan segera memutuskan kasus-kasus penting yang dapat mendefinisikan kekuasaan presiden. Kasus-kasus ini menyangkut batas-batas kekuasaan eksekutif, terutama yang kontroversial selama pemerintahan Trump. Mayoritas konservatif di Mahkamah Agung dapat mempengaruhi lingkup kekuasaan presiden di bidang keamanan nasional, imigrasi, dan lainnya, menjadi isu politik penting menjelang pemilu 2028.
topics.keywords: Supreme Court · presidential power · conservative majority · executive authority

topics.title: Deep State dan Pembersihan Badan Intelijen
topics.content: Aksi pemecatan yang disebut 'deep state' telah dimulai di badan intelijen tingkat atas AS. Direktur sementara yang ditunjuk presiden mungkin mendorong perubahan personel lebih lanjut, menimbulkan kekhawatiran tentang independensi badan intelijen. Konsep ini dipopulerkan oleh pendukung Trump, merujuk pada birokrasi yang dianggap menghalangi agenda presiden.
topics.keywords: deep state · intelligence agency · firing · temporary director

topics.title: Pengaruh AIPAC dalam Politik Dua Partai
topics.content: Komite Urusan Publik Amerika-Israel (AIPAC) telah menjadi titik kontroversi dalam pemilu baru-baru ini, terutama setelah peristiwa 7 Oktober 2024. AIPAC mengeluarkan dana besar melalui super PAC, mempengaruhi pemilihan pendahuluan Demokrat, dan bahkan menyebabkan kekalahan anggota Kongres Republik seperti Tom Massie. Aktivitasnya memperdalam perpecahan dalam kedua partai mengenai kebijakan Israel.
topics.keywords: AIPAC · super PAC · Israel · political rift · October 7th

quiz.question: Dalam kalimat asli 'Two are going up against Democratic incumbents', makna terdekat dari 'incumbents' adalah?
quiz.explanation: incumbent berarti pejabat petahana, di sini merujuk pada dua sosialis demokrat yang menantang anggota Kongres Demokrat petahana.

quiz.question: Dalam kalimat asli 'hoping to ride the wave of Democratic socialists', arti 'ride the wave' adalah?
quiz.explanation: ride the wave adalah ungkapan tetap yang berarti memanfaatkan tren atau momentum yang menguntungkan.

quiz.question: 原文 'super PACs at large who are spending millions of dollars in deceptive and misleading ads that are blanketing airwaves' 中，'blanketing' 的含义是？
quiz.explanation: blanket 作动词意为覆盖，这里指广告铺天盖地。

quiz.question: 根据对话，民主社会主义者在哪些地方取得了胜利？
options: B. 华盛顿特区和西雅图
quiz.explanation: Chuck Rocha提到华盛顿特区上周三位民主社会主义者逆袭，Audie Cornish补充西雅图也有胜利。

quiz.question: Letitia James如何描述民主社会主义者与进步派民主党人之间的分裂？
quiz.explanation: James引用马丁·路德·金的'创造性紧张'概念，认为这种分裂是推动社会正义的催化剂。

quiz.question: 关于AIPAC，以下哪项说法正确？
options: C. 对两党都造成分裂
quiz.explanation: Tamara Keith指出AIPAC在民主党和共和党内部都造成了政治裂痕，例如共和党议员Tom Massie的失利。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.text: And imagine opening your door and finding two million reasons to stay inside. That is exactly what happened in Texas after a truck carrying millions of bees crashed. Video from Queen Bee Supply shows the chaos workers faced to capture the swarm. The company says that professional and backyard beekeepers stepped in to help. That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. We're buzzing away. But CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: Bayangkan membuka pintu dan menemukan dua juta alasan untuk tinggal di rumah. Itulah yang terjadi di Texas, sebuah truk yang membawa jutaan lebah mengalami kecelakaan. Video dari Queen Bee Supply menunjukkan kekacauan saat para pekerja menangkap kawanan lebah. Perusahaan itu mengatakan bahwa peternak lebah profesional dan amatir turun tangan. CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS sampai di sini. Saya Brad Smith. Kami pergi dengan berdengung. Tapi CNN THIS MORNING bersama Audie Cornish dimulai sekarang.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Today in the group chat, it's primary day in New York. Can more Democratic socialists ride Mayor Mamdani's wave? MAYOR ZOHRAN MAMDANI (D),
paragraphs.cn: Hari ini dalam grup chat, ini adalah hari pemilihan pendahuluan di New York. Bisakah lebih banyak sosialis demokrat maju dengan gelombang Walikota Mandani? Walikota Zoran Mandani (Demokrat)

paragraphs.speaker: New York City
paragraphs.text: When does the race for 2028 begin? It starts now.
paragraphs.cn: Kapan kampanye 2028 dimulai? Mulai sekarang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Anticipation builds to today's Supreme Court decisions. Will justices go against the president in cases that could help define his power? And so-called deep-state firings have started at a top U.S. intelligence agency. What else could the president's hand-picked temporary director do? And Tucker Carlson says he's out. Will he take MAGA with him?
paragraphs.cn: Antisipasi terhadap keputusan Mahkamah Agung hari ini semakin meningkat. Akankah para hakim menentang presiden dalam kasus yang mungkin membantu mendefinisikan kekuasaan presiden? Tindakan pemecatan yang disebut 'deep state' telah dimulai di salah satu badan intelijen top AS. Apa lagi yang bisa dilakukan direktur sementara yang dipilih langsung oleh presiden? Tucker Carlson bilang dia keluar. Akankah dia membawa serta MAGA?

paragraphs.speaker: Tucker Carlson
paragraphs.text: I would not support the Republican Party. There's no chance I would support the Republican Party.
paragraphs.cn: Saya tidak akan mendukung Partai Republik. Sama sekali tidak mungkin saya mendukung Partai Republik.

paragraphs.speaker: Mamdani
paragraphs.text: Now, people often ask me what I think of the state of the Democratic Party. This slate here today is our answer. The Democratic Party must change.
paragraphs.cn: Sekarang, orang sering bertanya kepada saya bagaimana pandangan saya tentang keadaan Partai Demokrat saat ini. Daftar di sini hari ini adalah jawaban kami. Partai Demokrat harus berubah.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Zohran Mamdani putting his power to the test. Can his team of Democratic socialists win big in New York? Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish, and right now polls are open in New York. And the influence of New York City's mayor, tested just six months into his tenure. He's backed candidates in three House races. Two are going up against Democratic incumbents, and they are hoping to ride the wave of Democratic socialists, who have been winning big races across the country. BRAD LANDER (D),
paragraphs.cn: Zoran Mandani sedang menguji kekuatannya. Bisakah tim sosialis demokratnya menang besar di New York? Selamat pagi semuanya. Saya Audie Cornish, dan tempat pemungutan suara di New York sudah dibuka. Pengaruh walikota New York City, hanya enam bulan setelah menjabat, sedang diuji. Dia mendukung kandidat di tiga distrik DPR. Dua di antaranya bersaing dengan incumbent dari Partai Demokrat, mereka berharap maju dengan gelombang sosialis demokrat yang telah memenangkan pemilu besar di seluruh negeri. Brad Lander (Demokrat)

paragraphs.speaker: Candidate
paragraphs.text: I'm a longtime progressive, a Working Families Party member. But yes, if by "Democratic socialist," do you mean do I want Medicare for all? Do I want to make sure that folks can organize unions? Do I want to make sure we're getting to universal childcare and investing a lot more in housing everyone can afford? Yes. DARIALIZA AVILA CHEVALIER (D),
paragraphs.cn: Saya seorang progresif lama, anggota Partai Keluarga Pekerja. Tapi ya, jika 'sosialis demokrat' berarti apakah saya menginginkan perawatan kesehatan universal? Apakah saya ingin memastikan orang bisa membentuk serikat pekerja? Apakah saya ingin memastikan kita mewujudkan penitipan anak universal dan berinvestasi besar-besaran dalam perumahan yang terjangkau bagi semua orang? Ya. Daria Liza Avila Chevalier (Demokrat)

paragraphs.speaker: Candidate
paragraphs.text: I am someone who has felt deeply abandoned by the establishment politics that far too often see my community as merely a statistic.
paragraphs.cn: Saya adalah seseorang yang sangat dikecewakan oleh politik mapan yang sering memperlakukan komunitas saya hanya sebagai statistik.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right, so today in the group chat, Tamara Keith, senior political correspondent at NPR; Chuck Rocha, Democratic strategist and former senior adviser to Bernie Sanders's presidential campaigns; and Ashley Davis, former White House official under President George W. Bush. Love seeing you guys. Thank you so much for being here.
paragraphs.cn: Baik, hari ini dalam grup chat, ada jurnalis politik senior NPR Tamara Keith; ahli strategi Demokrat dan mantan penasihat senior kampanye presiden Bernie Sanders, Chuck Rocha; serta mantan pejabat Gedung Putih George W. Bush, Ashley Davis. Senang bertemu kalian. Terima kasih banyak sudah datang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, Tamara, I want to start with you, because Mamdani story is a story that I think a lot of these folks would like to emulate. They're not handpicked by the party, yet somehow, they rise via the -- you know, the Internet and the power of their ideas. And he is trying to have, like, tailwinds. So, do we know if he does?
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, Tamara, saya ingin bertanya kepadamu terlebih dahulu, karena kisah Mandani adalah sesuatu yang ingin ditiru banyak orang. Mereka tidak dipilih oleh partai, tetapi entah bagaimana mereka bangkit melalui kekuatan internet dan ide. Dia mencoba mendapatkan angin segar. Jadi, apakah kita tahu apakah dia berhasil?

paragraphs.speaker: Tamara Keith
paragraphs.text: I think we will know very soon whether he does. He -- certainly, some of these candidates look to be polling pretty well in their races. I don't know that we can extrapolate about the national Democratic Party from what happens in New York City.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir kita akan segera tahu. Dia -- -- tentu saja, beberapa kandidat ini memiliki jajak pendapat yang cukup baik dalam kampanye mereka. Saya tidak tahu apakah kita bisa menyimpulkan dari apa yang terjadi di New York City tentang keadaan Partai Demokrat secara nasional.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: No, I mean, Democratic socialists.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, maksud saya, sosialis demokrat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Which, city by city, I mean, even here in
paragraphs.cn: Setiap kota, maksud saya, bahkan di sini -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- we've been talking about that. I was noticing in -- was that Lander who we just played earlier?
paragraphs.cn: Kita sudah membahas ini. Saya perhatikan -- -- itu Lander yang baru kita putar?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Notice how the first thing out of his mouth in that answer is, "If by Democratic socialist, you mean." And I hear this over and over again, people trying to define it for themselves, even as actual Democratic socialists are out there defining it pretty loudly.
paragraphs.cn: Perhatikan kalimat pertama yang keluar saat dia menjawab: 'Jika sosialis demokrat berarti...' Saya mendengar ini berulang kali, orang mencoba mendefinisikannya sendiri, bahkan ketika sosialis demokrat yang sebenarnya mendefinisikannya dengan lantang.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: I would go a step further and say that it's already seen. In political world, you don't beat incumbents. You don't go against the machine. In city politics, New York City. To your point, Audie, in Washington, D.C., just last week, when three Democratic socialists overcame the odds in like --
paragraphs.cn: Saya ingin melangkah lebih jauh dengan mengatakan bahwa ini sudah terlihat. Di dunia politik, Anda tidak mengalahkan incumbent. Anda tidak melawan sistem. Dalam politik kota, New York City. Seperti yang dikatakan Audie, di Washington DC, minggu lalu, ketika tiga sosialis demokrat mengatasi rintangan -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: but that's much different than every other battleground district in America. There's about 34 battleground districts, and none of those are in the heart of any city where Democratic socialists -- And that's what the party is worried about. The Democratic socialist message of being for the people, by the people. Working class. Pull yourself up. That's all good. But in these marginal seats, they'll say everybody's a Democratic socialist. All Ashley's friends will be running ads tomorrow on this.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi ini sangat berbeda dari setiap distrik medan pertempuran lainnya di Amerika. Ada sekitar 34 distrik medan pertempuran, tidak satu pun yang berada di pusat kota tempat sosialis demokrat berada -- -- dan itulah yang dikhawatirkan partai. Pesan 'dari rakyat, oleh rakyat, untuk rakyat' dari sosialis demokrat, kelas pekerja, mandiri, semuanya bagus. Tapi di kursi-kursi pinggiran ini, mereka akan mengatakan semua orang adalah sosialis demokrat. Semua teman Ashley akan memasang iklan untuk itu besok.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I know. You're trying to divorce it here. I want to play for you. This is Attorney General Tish James.
paragraphs.cn: Saya tahu. Anda ingin menjauhkan diri di sini. Saya ingin memutar klip untuk Anda. Ini adalah Jaksa Agung Tish James.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: -- with it. I ain't divorcing nothing.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- dan itu. Saya tidak menjauhkan diri dari apa pun.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: No, no, hold on one second, because this is going to be the difficult thing as a consultant.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, tidak, tunggu, karena sebagai konsultan, ini akan menjadi hal yang sulit.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: How do you do this when there are people who are successfully running --
paragraphs.cn: Ketika seseorang berhasil beroperasi, bagaimana Anda melakukannya -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- behind this flag? So basically, race was -- Vox was calling this race a splitting of Mamdani's coalition: that it pitted progressives against Democratic socialists. And then Letitia James was asked about this split. She's the former [SIC] attorney general. Here's what she had to say.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- di balik bendera ini? Jadi pada dasarnya, kampanye ini -- -- Vox menyebut kampanye ini sebagai perpecahan koalisi Mandani: ini mempertentangkan progresif dengan sosialis demokrat. Kemudian Letitia James ditanya tentang perpecahan ini. Dia adalah mantan [sic] jaksa agung. Berikut pernyataannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Letitia James
paragraphs.text: What we are witnessing here at this moment is the tension. Dr. King talked about creative tension being a catalyst for social -- for social justice. And clearly, given D.S. -- DSA and quote, unquote "progressive Democrats," we are moving forward, and we are broadening the tent of the Democratic Party. We are moving forward, broadening the tent, focusing on women, people of color, immigrants, working people in the labor movement.
paragraphs.cn: What we are witnessing here is tension. Dr. King talked about creative tension as a catalyst for social justice. Clearly, given the D.S. -- -- DSA and the so-called "progressive Democrats," we are moving forward, we are expanding the Democratic tent. We are moving forward, expanding the tent, focusing on women, people of color, immigrants, and workers in the labor movement.

paragraphs.speaker: Letitia James
paragraphs.text: And that's why -- that's what you are witnessing now, given the split between a mayor who I've supported and who is a friend of mine, who I respect.
paragraphs.cn: That is why -- -- this is what you are witnessing now, given the split between a mayor I once supported, who is also my friend and whom I respect.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And I should say current attorney general.
paragraphs.cn: I should say the current Attorney General.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But I wanted to ask you how you hear her describing this split, which I think Bush-era Republicans may be familiar with. Right? When the party starts to pull away from you.
paragraphs.cn: But I want to ask you, how do you understand this split she describes? I think Republicans from the Bush era might be familiar with this. Right? When the party starts to move away from you.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Well, right. I mean, I think this whole movement is two things. One, it's the Tea Party of the Republicans back in the day, or maybe even MAGA in some way. But also, I think it's really anti-establishment, which most people in the country, whether you are Republicans or Democrats, are 100 percent running against the status quo, because no one's happy with it. But what's really going to show me for the mayor, because I think that these -- he has a really good chance of winning these three races today.
paragraphs.cn: Well, right. I mean, I think there are two aspects to the whole movement. One is the Tea Party of the Republican Party back then, or even to some extent MAGA. But I also think it's really anti-establishment, and most people across the country, whether you are Republican or Democrat, are 100% against the status quo because no one is satisfied with it. But what really caught my attention about the mayor is because I think these -- -- he has a good chance of winning these three races today.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: At least two of them. But can they win in a general? And I think that's the question.
paragraphs.cn: At least two of them. But can they win the general election? I think that's a question.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: I think we'll see. Some will, some won't.
paragraphs.cn: I think we'll see. Some will, some won't.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Let me just play one last thing. This is the mayor. Mayor Zohran Mamdani. He was just speaking from the mayor's office on Monday.
paragraphs.cn: One more clip. This is Mayor Zoran Mamdani. He just spoke from the mayor's office on Monday.

paragraphs.speaker: Mamdani
paragraphs.text: I was quoting Gramsci, who said that the old world is dying and the new world struggles to be born. Now is the time of monsters. I used the term to describe all those who are preventing the birth of a new world. Not solely AIPAC, but frankly, super PACs at large who are spending millions of dollars in deceptive and misleading ads that are blanketing airwaves.
paragraphs.cn: I quote Gramsci, who said the old world is dying, and the new world struggles to be born. Now is the time of monsters. I use this term to describe all those who hinder the birth of the new world. Not just AIPAC, but frankly, also the super PACs that spend millions of dollars on deceptive and misleading ads flooding the airwaves.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I wanted to add this one last thing. We're going to talk about it more in the program. But this conversation around AIPAC and how it is dividing the Democratic Party and, to some extent, even Republicans. I remember Tom Massie's loss in Kentucky. He vehemently criticized AIPAC after.
paragraphs.cn: I want to add one last point. We'll discuss this further on the show. But the conversation around AIPAC and how it splits the Democratic Party, and even to some extent the Republican Party. I remember Thomas Massie's loss in Kentucky. He strongly criticized AIPAC afterward.

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.text: Yes. And AIPAC has, in the last several years, become a real boogeyman in races. This obviously has a lot to do with politics involving Israel and Israel's shift --
paragraphs.cn: Yes. In the past few years, AIPAC has really become a villain in elections. This clearly has a lot to do with the politics involving Israel and the shift in Israel -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And exacerbated by October 7th and all that has followed since.
paragraphs.cn: And it's been exacerbated by the events of October 7th and everything since.

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.text: Absolutely. And everything that has followed.
paragraphs.cn: Absolutely. And everything that followed.

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.text: And it has created a political rift, both in the Democratic Party and in the Republican Party. And this backlash, you know, against Israel, which is a change.
paragraphs.cn: This has created political rifts within both the Democratic and Republican parties. This backlash against Israel is a change.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. And that thread runs through Tucker Carlson's exit, as --
paragraphs.cn: Yes. This thread runs through Tucker Carlson's departure -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.text: It runs all the way from Tucker Carlson to Zohran Mamdani.
paragraphs.cn: From Tucker Carlson all the way to Zoran Mamdani.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Exactly. So, we're going to talk more about that on the program today. We've got a lot to handle. I do want to bring up one other thing, coming up on CNN THIS MORNING. There's actually a new clue in the Nancy Guthrie case. What it could say about her disappearance. Plus, what's behind the tarp outside the Kennedy Center? New images showing what we now know is not there. And we're remembering the man with the golden ears who launched the careers of icons, including Whitney Houston and Bruce Springsteen.
paragraphs.cn: That's right. So, we'll discuss this further on today's show. We have a lot to cover. I also want to mention another thing that will air on CNN This Morning. There is actually a new lead in the Nancy Guthrie case. It might explain her disappearance. Also, what's behind the tarp at the Kennedy Center? New images show we now know what's not there. We are also remembering the man with the golden ear who launched the careers of icons including Whitney Houston and Bruce Springsteen.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Legendary music producer Clive Davis has died at the age of 94. Here he is in 2024, talking about his legacy.
paragraphs.cn: Legendary music producer Clive Davis has died at the age of 94. This is footage of him in 2024 talking about his legacy.

paragraphs.speaker: Clive Davis
paragraphs.text: I discovered or nurtured an unusual array of the most gifted artists of all time and that they felt safe.
paragraphs.cn: I discovered or nurtured some of the most outstanding artists of all time, and they felt safe.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It is now 12 minutes past the hour. This is your morning roundup. Law enforcement sources telling CNN a note connected to the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie claims that she died, and that note was sent to media outlets back in February. We're told the kidnappers wrote it -- wrote that she died shortly after she was taken and that they did not mean to kill her. Now, this note followed one prior which demanded ransom. Investigators believe these notes are legitimate. And a major ruling on voting and privacy.
paragraphs.cn: It's 12 minutes past the hour. Here is your morning news summary. Law enforcement sources tell CNN that a note related to Nancy Guthrie's disappearance claims she is dead, and that note was sent to the media in February. We are told the kidnapper wrote -- -- she died shortly after being taken, and they did not intend to kill her. This note came after a previous ransom note. Investigators believe these notes are authentic. Also, a major ruling on voting and privacy.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The Trump administration blocked from using Social Security and immigration data to scrub voter rolls. A federal judge says the system relied on inaccurate information and risks wrongly removing eligible voters. Now, this ruling halted a key part of the president -- of the president's election overhaul plan. And we're getting a new look at what's behind the tarp at the Kennedy Center. These images, obtained by CNN, show President Trump's name is no longer on the building.
paragraphs.cn: The Trump administration has been barred from using Social Security and immigration data to clean voter rolls. A federal judge said the system relies on inaccurate information and risks wrongly removing eligible voters. This ruling halts a key part of the president's election reform plan. We also saw new images of what's behind the Kennedy Center curtain. These images obtained by CNN show that President Trump's name is no longer on the building.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Workers began removing Trump's name from an exterior wall ten days ago, complying with a judge's ruling that the institution could only be named for John F. Kennedy as a living memorial to the late president. Ashley, I feel like you have some goss here. You're involved with the National Symphony?
paragraphs.cn: Workers began removing Trump's name from the facade ten days ago to comply with a judge's ruling that the institution can only be named after John F. Kennedy, as a living memorial to the late president. Ashley, I think you have some gossip here. Are you connected to the National Symphony Orchestra?

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Yes, absolutely. And I think it's really important, because the Kennedy Center -- also, the judge said the Kennedy Center will remain open for now, as well. And we'll see what happens there.
paragraphs.cn: Yes, of course. I think this is very important because the Kennedy Center -- -- and the judge said the Kennedy Center will remain open for now. We'll see.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Instead of shutting down for a big Trump-induced renovation.
paragraphs.cn: Rather than closing due to major renovations caused by Trump.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Exactly. But the NSO is in to stay. And they are either going to do it with the symphony or some other way, if there's not going to be a budget to support them. But 100 percent, these musicians have stayed with the NSO from the beginning, and they --
paragraphs.cn: Benar. Tapi National Symphony Orchestra akan tetap ada. Mereka akan bekerja sama dengan orkestra atau dengan cara lain, jika tidak ada dukungan anggaran untuk mereka. Tapi 100%, para musisi ini bersama National Symphony Orchestra sejak awal, dan mereka --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: None of them quit during --
paragraphs.cn: Tidak ada yang mengundurkan diri selama ini --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- this big upheaval.
paragraphs.cn: -- dalam kekacauan besar ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: And we have a season to announce as soon as we can, and --
paragraphs.cn: Kami akan segera mengumumkan jadwal pertunjukan musim baru, dan --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: National Symphony Orchestra.
paragraphs.cn: National Symphony Orchestra.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: That's why I don't know what the NSO is.
paragraphs.cn: Makanya saya tidak tahu apa itu NSO.

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Cornish
paragraphs.text: I'm going to take you to a concert. I'm going to take you to a concert.
paragraphs.cn: Saya akan mengajak Anda ke konser. Saya akan mengajak Anda ke konser.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Shout-out to the National Symphony orchestra. Glad you guys stuck with it. Looking forward to the new season. I want to talk about this after the break. Does a quieter Fed equal a louder market? How Kevin Warsh's gamble might impact your money. Plus, the light at the end of the tunnel is here for Americans exposed on that hantavirus-stricken cruise ship. In the meantime, a live look in Houston. The World Cup play will continue there with a match featuring Portugal versus Uzbekistan.
paragraphs.cn: Salut untuk National Symphony Orchestra. Senang kalian bertahan. Menantikan musim baru. Saya ingin membahas topik ini setelah istirahat. Apakah Federal Reserve yang lebih tenang berarti pasar yang lebih ribut? Bagaimana taruhan Kevin Warsh akan mempengaruhi uang Anda? Selain itu, cahaya di ujung terowongan telah muncul bagi warga Amerika yang terpapar virus Hantavirus di kapal pesiar. Sementara itu, gambar langsung dari Houston. Pertandingan Piala Dunia akan terus berlangsung di sana, Portugal melawan Uzbekistan.

paragraphs.speaker: Kevin Warsh
paragraphs.text: I don't share the view that was expressed a few generations ago that Federal Reserve chairmen show up at a podium like this and say, you've got to choose, and you're going to have to decide whether you're willing to tolerate higher inflation to put more people at work.
paragraphs.cn: Saya tidak setuju dengan pandangan yang diungkapkan beberapa generasi lalu, bahwa ketua Federal Reserve berdiri di podium seperti ini dan berkata, Anda harus memilih, Anda harus memutuskan apakah Anda bersedia mentolerir inflasi yang lebih tinggi untuk mempekerjakan lebih banyak orang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Newly sworn-in Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh, leaving interest rates steady for now. But concerning economic data, since the start of the war with Iran could mean he'll have to change his mind. So let me give you an example. Inflation rose half a percent from April to May. That means it's now around 4.2 percent. Consumer confidence dipping among Americans. So, joining me now Courtenay Brown. She's the senior economics reporter at Axios. Welcome back. Thanks so much.
paragraphs.cn: Ketua Federal Reserve yang baru, Kevin Warsh, dilantik dan untuk sementara mempertahankan suku bunga tidak berubah. Tapi data ekonomi sejak perang dengan Iran mungkin berarti dia harus berubah pikiran. Contohnya, tingkat inflasi naik 0,5 poin persentase dari April ke Mei, dan saat ini sekitar 4,2%. Kepercayaan konsumen AS menurun. Sekarang mari kita sambut Courtney Brown, jurnalis ekonomi senior Axios. Selamat datang kembali, terima kasih banyak.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, I'm starting with the inflation, just to give people a sense, because that's always the boogeyman. Is inflation high? Is inflation low? And this is where monetary policy comes into play. What is our sense, now that Warsh is in the job, of how he thinks about these things?
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, saya akan mulai dengan inflasi, untuk memberi gambaran, karena inflasi selalu menjadi hal yang menakutkan. Apakah inflasi tinggi? Apakah inflasi rendah? Di sinilah kebijakan moneter berperan. Sekarang Warsh sudah menjabat, apa yang kita ketahui tentang bagaimana dia memandang masalah ini?

paragraphs.speaker: Courtenay Brown
paragraphs.text: Do you want to hear something crazy?
paragraphs.cn: Anda ingin mendengar sesuatu yang gila?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: About Fed policy, Courtenay. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: Tentang kebijakan Federal Reserve, Courtney. Ya.

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.text: Because he is not so -- Warsh's big audition was last week. First Fed policy meeting. We get the closely parsed policy statement. It is 130 words, more than a third of the length in the Powell era, just months before. He wants to say less. He wants to guide markets less. What we know is that he said that inflation -- or the Fed will accomplish price stability. A wonky way of saying they will get inflation back to 2 percent. As you said, so far from 2 percent right now.
paragraphs.cn: Karena dia tidak begitu -- Ujian besar Warsh adalah minggu lalu. Rapat kebijakan Federal Reserve pertama. Kami mendapatkan pernyataan kebijakan yang dibaca dengan cermat. Hanya 130 kata, lebih dari sepertiga panjang era Powell, hanya beberapa bulan lalu. Dia ingin berbicara lebih sedikit. Dia ingin mengurangi panduan ke pasar. Yang kami tahu adalah dia mengatakan inflasi -- atau Federal Reserve akan mencapai stabilitas harga. Itu cara berbelit-belit untuk mengatakan mereka akan menurunkan inflasi kembali ke 2%. Seperti yang Anda katakan, sekarang masih jauh dari 2%.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Can I -- let's look at the chart. She was making fun of me trying to read my own charts here for business.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Boleh saya -- Mari kita lihat grafik ini. Dia baru saja menertawakan saya karena mencoba membaca grafik bisnis saya sendiri di sini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Consumer price index.
paragraphs.cn: Indeks Harga Konsumen.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. What are we looking at in this chart right now?
paragraphs.cn: Oke. Apa yang kita lihat di grafik ini sekarang?

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.text: So, what we've seen is inflation shooting right up since the war.
paragraphs.cn: Kita melihat bahwa tingkat inflasi meningkat tajam sejak perang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Oh, see, I did the same thing guys. That's confidence. Give me consumer price.
paragraphs.cn: Oh, lihat, saya juga melakukan hal yang sama, teman-teman. Inilah kepercayaan diri. Beri saya harga konsumen.

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.text: But that's an important story, too.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi ini juga topik yang penting.

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.text: The stories are connected.
paragraphs.cn: Topik-topik ini saling terkait.

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.text: Because inflation is shooting up, consumers feel really crappy about the economy right now. We all know this. Not a new story. It is a -- it is partially because of inflation, but it's also because of price levels. Prices have just been really high and have not come down. So, I think what the Fed has to grapple with here is how do you bring inflation down without wrecking the economy? Financial markets have decided that the Fed will probably raise interest rates this year. That's crazy.
paragraphs.cn: Karena inflasi meningkat tajam, konsumen sekarang merasa sangat buruk tentang ekonomi. Kita semua tahu itu. Ini bukan hal baru. Sebagian karena inflasi, tapi juga karena tingkat harga. Harga tetap tinggi dan tidak turun. Jadi, saya pikir masalah yang harus dihadapi Federal Reserve adalah, bagaimana menurunkan inflasi tanpa meruntuhkan ekonomi? Pasar keuangan sudah memutuskan bahwa Federal Reserve mungkin akan menaikkan suku bunga tahun ini. Ini gila.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: "Wall Street Journal" saying Fed could raise -- could raise it three times this year.
paragraphs.cn: The Wall Street Journal mengatakan Federal Reserve mungkin menaikkan suku bunga tiga kali tahun ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.text: Everyone on Wall Street --
paragraphs.cn: Semua orang di Wall Street --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- reporting from the forecast from Bank of America.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 都在报道美国银行的预测。

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.text: Everyone on Wall Street is revising their expectations for rate hikes this year. When just a few months ago, they were expecting rate cuts. But we don't actually know. Kevin Warsh didn't say that. He doesn't want to guide markets in the way that traditional Fed chairman have. He wants to go back to the Greenspan era, the late Alan Greenspan, who died yesterday.
paragraphs.cn: 华尔街的每个人都在修正他们对今年加息的预期。而就在几个月前，他们还预期会降息。但我们实际上并不知道。凯文·沃什没有这么说。他不想像传统美联储主席那样引导市场。他想回到格林斯潘时代，即已故的艾伦·格林斯潘，他昨天去世了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Let's take a beat on that.
paragraphs.cn: 让我们先缓一缓。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Because there's some familiar language here. When the -- the late Alan Greenspan, who died this week, when he took over the Fed from Paul Volcker, you had Gulf wars fueling spikes in prices, right? So, something going on in the Middle East. You had high inflation, and you had real worries about that and prices. So, some of this feels a bit familiar for us in this moment under Trump's term. What did Greenspan do that's part of his legacy, why people still think of him with -- with such reverence?
paragraphs.cn: 因为这里有一些熟悉的说法。当已故的艾伦·格林斯潘（本周去世）从保罗·沃尔克手中接管美联储时，海湾战争导致价格飙升，对吧？所以，中东发生了一些事情。当时通胀高企，人们对此和物价感到切实担忧。所以，在特朗普任期的此刻，我们感觉有些似曾相识。格林斯潘做了什么，成为他遗产的一部分，为什么人们至今仍对他如此崇敬？

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.text: The celebrity Fed chairman of our era, really. There's one more parallel to this moment that's quite spooky. Alan Greenspan, during his term, he presided -- or he led the Fed over a period where there was huge technological change, the Internet boom of the '90s.
paragraphs.cn: 他真是我们这个时代的名人美联储主席。还有一个与此刻非常诡异的相似之处。艾伦·格林斯潘在任期间，他主持 --  -- 或者说领导美联储度过了一个技术巨变的时期，即90年代的互联网繁荣。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Oh, yes. And its bubble.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，是的。还有它的泡沫。

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.text: Yes. Sound like A.I. a little bit right now?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。听起来有点像现在的人工智能？

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.text: Maybe. So, I think Alan Greenspan is having such a moment right now. It is so interesting to me that he died at the -- at the moment where his legacy is kind of front and center --
paragraphs.cn: 也许吧。所以，我认为艾伦·格林斯潘此刻正备受关注。我觉得非常有趣的是，他去世的时刻，他的遗产正好处于焦点位置 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.text: -- among Trump economic officials. You hear them shouting out Alan Greenspan because of what he did. He -- he believed that, then the Internet boom was going to usher in this huge era of productivity. And it would not be inflationary. And we've heard the White House explicitly say that they think the Fed can do the very same thing in this moment: keep interest rates low, not cut off this economic boom that we're having.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 在特朗普的经济官员中。你听到他们大声赞扬艾伦·格林斯潘，因为他所做的事。他相信互联网繁荣将带来一个巨大的生产力时代，而且不会引发通胀。我们听到白宫明确表示，他们认为美联储此刻可以做同样的事情：保持低利率，不切断我们正在经历的经济繁荣。

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.text: But the problem is exactly what you say. We have this inflation issue. It's really hard for the Fed not to raise interest rates in the face of inflation that is soaring now, but was already sticky before the war. So, they have a problem.
paragraphs.cn: 但问题正如你所说。我们面临通胀问题。面对目前飙升的通胀 --  -- 尽管在战争前就已经居高不下 --  -- 美联储很难不加息。所以，他们面临一个难题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: One other thing I want to read from Alan Greenspan, because I think it's relevant here. In 1994, he said, "We will be at price stability when households and businesses need not factor expectations of changes in the average level of prices into their decisions." I'm going to translate and say he cared about our prices, about how households were able to afford their economy.
paragraphs.cn: 我还想引用艾伦·格林斯潘的一句话，因为我认为这与此相关。1994年，他说："当家庭和企业无需将平均价格水平变化的预期纳入决策时，我们就实现了价格稳定。"我翻译一下，意思是他关心我们的物价，关心家庭如何负担得起他们的经济生活。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Courtenay, thank you so much. Love having you. I'm going to come up with more excuses to cover Fed policy just to soak in your enthusiasm. Next on CNN THIS MORNING, we're going to talk about the issues from transgender athletes to birthright citizenship before the Supreme Court. We could get some opinions later today, before its summer recess. Plus, mass firings underway at the nation's top intel office. And lawmakers warn of serious risks with each lost job. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 考特尼，非常感谢你。很高兴你能来。我会找更多借口来报道美联储政策，只为沉浸在你的热情中。接下来在《CNN今晨》节目中，我们将讨论最高法院审理的从跨性别运动员到出生公民权等问题。今天晚些时候，在最高法院夏季休庭前，我们可能会得到一些意见。此外，国家最高情报机构正在进行大规模裁员。议员们警告说，每失去一个工作岗位都伴随着严重风险。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. Thank you for joining me on CNN THIS MORNING. It is half past the hour. And here's what's happening right now. Secretary of State Marco Rubio heads to the Middle East. He is the first White House official to do so after the signing of the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding. Rubio will be in the region discussing that deal, along with, quote, regional priorities, including the Strait of Hormuz.
paragraphs.cn: 大家早上好。我是奥蒂·科尼什。感谢您收看《CNN今晨》节目。现在是半点钟。以下是正在发生的事情。国务卿马可·卢比奥前往中东。他是美伊谅解备忘录签署后首位前往该地区的白宫官员。卢比奥将在该地区讨论该协议，以及所谓的"地区优先事项"，包括霍尔木兹海峡。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: And quarantine finally over for the last eight American passengers exposed to hantavirus who were staying at a Nebraska facility. Now, they were exposed to that virus on a cruise ship early last month. The passengers stayed in the quarantine unit for 42 days. And Messi makes history again. He scored two goals in Argentina's win over Austria, officially earning him the most goals scored in World Cup history, 18.
paragraphs.cn: 最后八名接触过汉坦病毒的美国乘客终于结束了隔离，他们一直住在内布拉斯加州的一家设施内。这些乘客上月初在一艘游轮上接触了该病毒。他们在隔离区待了42天。梅西再次创造历史。他在阿根廷战胜奥地利的比赛中攻入两球，正式成为世界杯历史进球最多的球员，共18球。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Messi. Messi's a beast. Messi came to get the record of most goals in the World Cup, and he got it. He got two of them.
paragraphs.cn: 梅西。梅西是个猛兽。梅西来打破世界杯进球纪录，他做到了。他进了两个球。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But Messi will be fighting to keep the record. France, on the right, on his heels with 16 all-time goals. And this morning the Supreme Court expected to release some opinions for its outstanding docket. So, you've got cases still left outstanding, those include birthright citizenship. Also, presidential firing authority and transgender athletes. These are all opinions the Trump administration has a vested interest in.
paragraphs.cn: 但梅西将努力保持这一纪录。法国队以16个总进球紧随其后。今天上午，最高法院预计将就其未决案件发布一些意见。目前仍有未决案件，包括出生公民权、总统解雇权以及跨性别运动员。特朗普政府对这些意见都有切身利益。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And Trump has already lashed out against the Supreme Court over decisions relating to, say, his tariff policy. So, could these opinions further erode the relationship between the executive and judiciary? The group chat is back. The big one here I want to mention is birthright citizenship. This could really be something that would annoy Trump. And, honestly, it's not looking that good because it's one of those things where it's kind of in the Constitution.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普已经就关税政策等相关裁决抨击过最高法院。那么，这些意见是否会进一步损害行政与司法部门之间的关系？群聊又回来了。我想提的一个重要问题是出生公民权。这可能会让特朗普感到恼火。老实说，情况看起来不太乐观，因为这是宪法中有所规定的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: What are you thinking about how he's feeling? TAMARA KEITH, HOST, "NPR POLITICS PODCAST": Yes, it was one of his day one priorities in his second term. It's something that he campaigned on. It's also something that legal experts have been extremely dubious about the entire time.
paragraphs.cn: 你觉得他现在感觉如何？塔玛拉·基思（《NPR政治播客》主持人）：是的，这是他第二任期首日的优先事项之一。这是他竞选时承诺的。同时，法律专家一直对此深表怀疑。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.text: And I do think that we could expect him, if it goes against him, to be very unhappy and not to be quiet about that because he is never quiet when he is unhappy. And you saw that with the tariff ruling. And, you know, like the relationship between --
paragraphs.cn: 我确实认为，如果裁决对他不利，我们可以预期他会非常不满，并且不会保持沉默，因为他从不掩饰自己的不满。你在关税裁决中已经看到了这一点。而且，你知道，就像行政与司法之间的关系 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.text: I mean, like even talking about it as a relationship anymore --
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，甚至再把它当作一段关系来谈论 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But even many of these things, out of all the -- everything on the list, I feel like the transgender athlete case is the one that becomes a talking point in elections, right? In this case, it's two students challenging state laws, banning participation in -- their participation in girl's sports, arguing that it violates Title IX. Whereas like, the abortion ruling, so many years ago, became this, like, pivotal thing for Democrats.
paragraphs.cn: 但即使是这些事情中的许多，在清单上的所有事情中，我觉得跨性别运动员案件会成为选举中的一个话题，对吧？在这个案件中，两名学生挑战州法律，禁止她们参加女子体育项目，认为这违反了《教育法修正案第九条》。而多年前的堕胎裁决，则成了民主党人的一个关键议题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Out of everything here, mail-in ballots, birthright citizenship, it feels like you're going to see ads about a transgender athlete ruling.
paragraphs.cn: 在所有这些事情中，邮寄选票、出生公民权，感觉你会看到关于跨性别运动员裁决的广告。

paragraphs.speaker: Ashley Davis
paragraphs.text: It only becomes an issue if they say that you can have the transgender athletes in the sports. I think that if they say, no, you can't, I don't know, are Democrats going to defend that?
paragraphs.cn: 只有当他们说你可以让跨性别运动员参加体育比赛时，这才成为一个问题。我认为如果他们说不行，我不知道，民主党人会为此辩护吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Chuck Rocha
paragraphs.text: I think that you're already seeing Republicans use transgender, whether or not the Supreme Court does this or not, you're going to see two types of ads, and we're making hundreds of ads right now in these congressional seats. And you have Democrats talking about cost of living, and you have Republicans talking about trans athletes or somebody having low t. That's going to be the argument of -- we're seeing what the midterms --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为你已经看到共和党人在利用跨性别议题，无论最高法院是否这样做，你都会看到两种类型的广告，我们现在正在这些国会选区制作数百个广告。民主党人在谈论生活成本，而共和党人在谈论跨性别运动员或某人睾酮水平低。这将成为争论的焦点 --  -- 我们正在看到中期选举的情况 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Low t. The low t, testosterone, conversation.
paragraphs.cn: Tingkat testosteron rendah. Diskusi tentang testosteron rendah.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: I like that. Do you like that?
paragraphs.cn: Saya suka ini. Apakah kamu suka?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Why is it in politics?
paragraphs.cn: Mengapa ini muncul dalam politik?

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: I'm just telling you what they got.
paragraphs.cn: Saya hanya memberi tahu Anda apa yang mereka miliki.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It's like, guys, just save it for your supplements. I don't need --
paragraphs.cn: Seperti, teman-teman, serahkan pada suplemen Anda. Saya tidak perlu -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: If you ain't got nothing else. (CROSS TALK).
paragraphs.cn: Jika Anda tidak punya yang lain. (Percakapan silang)

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: They're talking about low t?
paragraphs.cn: Apakah mereka membicarakan testosteron rendah?

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: I'm just saying, as a boy who takes a lot of protein, this is what they're doing and it's going to lose momentum (ph).
paragraphs.cn: Saya hanya mengatakan, sebagai seorang anak laki-laki yang mengonsumsi banyak protein, mereka melakukan ini, tapi ini akan kehilangan momentum.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That's what they're calling Talarico. That's what they're calling Talarico. Which I get, but just the whole --
paragraphs.cn: Mereka memanggil Talarico seperti itu. Mereka memanggil Talarico seperti itu. Saya mengerti, tapi seluruh -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: But it worked really well last time. I don't know if it's still the same.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi terakhir kali berhasil. Saya tidak tahu apakah sekarang masih sama.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: It's a different time.
paragraphs.cn: Zamannya berbeda.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Unless there's a bad ruling or a --
paragraphs.cn: Kecuali ada keputusan yang buruk atau -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: People are sick and tired right now. They're sick and tired of these prices. I just went home to see my mama this weekend for her 74th birthday, and only anything could say is that folks can't afford stuff.
paragraphs.cn: Orang-orang sekarang bosan. Mereka bosan dengan harga-harga ini. Akhir pekan ini saya pulang ke rumah ibu saya untuk merayakan ulang tahunnya yang ke-74, satu-satunya yang bisa dikatakan adalah orang-orang tidak mampu membeli barang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But, Chuck, isn't the argument that Democrats, too, are not focusing on the right things because they are focused on their affinity, communities or not eating steak.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi, Chuck, bukankah ada yang berpikir bahwa Demokrat juga tidak fokus pada hal yang benar, karena mereka fokus pada afinitas, komunitas, atau tidak makan steak?

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: Smart people like you want to talk about focus.
paragraphs.cn: Orang pintar sepertimu ingin membicarakan fokus.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: No, no, what I'm saying is that's --
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, tidak, maksud saya -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: I'm talking about ads.
paragraphs.cn: Saya sedang bicara tentang iklan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, don't ads generate focus? You -- they're determining where to look.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, bukankah iklan menciptakan fokus? Kamu -- -- Iklan menentukan ke mana orang melihat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And I think Republicans are going to say, don't look over here where they're talking about affordability, look over here.
paragraphs.cn: Dan saya pikir Partai Republik akan mengatakan ini: Jangan lihat ke sini, mereka bicara tentang keterjangkauan, lihat ke sini.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: You're right. That's right.
paragraphs.cn: Kamu benar. Benar.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: But that's what they're running against Talarico with your buddy on is they're going to go over some of these issues --
paragraphs.cn: Tapi begitulah cara mereka melawan Talarico bersama temanmu -- -- mereka akan membicarakan beberapa masalah ini -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: Because they can't talk about what Audie just said, which is the cost of things.
paragraphs.cn: Karena mereka tidak bisa membicarakan apa yang baru saja dikatakan Audie, yaitu harga.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: You can't talk about that. But you talk about something else.
paragraphs.cn: Kamu tidak bisa bicara tentang itu. Tapi kamu bisa bicara tentang hal lain.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Some of those ads are effective, I'm sorry to say.
paragraphs.cn: Beberapa iklan efektif, saya menyesal mengatakannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: Some of them with the base.
paragraphs.cn: Beberapa di antaranya ditujukan untuk hal-hal dasar.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Well, one other thing I want to add is, there is this idea, a Senate committee advancing a bipartisan bill to allow oral arguments to be televised, saying that this would be good for transparency. "The Washington Post" editorial board joining them. Or, sorry, "The Washington Post" editorial board complaining that this will contribute to grandstanding. You're laughing. I mean, I have to admit, I'm good with audio.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, saya ingin menambahkan satu hal. Ada pandangan bahwa sebuah komite Senat sedang mendorong undang-undang bipartisan yang mengizinkan siaran televisi dari argumen lisan, dengan alasan bahwa hal itu akan meningkatkan transparansi. Dewan Redaksi The Washington Post juga ikut serta. Atau, maaf, Dewan Redaksi The Washington Post mengeluh bahwa hal itu akan menyebabkan pertunjukan. Anda tertawa. Maksud saya, saya harus mengakui, saya mendukung audio.

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.text: I -- yes, I'm, as a (INAUDIBLE), I'm thrilled with the audio.
paragraphs.cn: Saya  --  --  ya, sebagai (tidak jelas), saya bersemangat tentang audio.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: As audio people, we're happy already.
paragraphs.cn: Sebagai orang audio, kami sudah senang.

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.text: Very happy already. I don't know that you can grandstand in the Supreme Court because I don't think the justices will stand for that. They are asking deep dive questions about very specific things.
paragraphs.cn: Sudah sangat senang. Saya tidak tahu apakah pertunjukan bisa dilakukan di Mahkamah Agung, karena saya pikir para hakim tidak akan mentolerirnya. Mereka akan mengajukan pertanyaan mendalam tentang isu-isu yang sangat spesifik.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But I think what they can -- what they say can be clipped and used against people in election slop. Like, I don't think --
paragraphs.cn: Tapi saya pikir apa yang mereka katakan bisa diedit dan digunakan untuk menyerang orang lain dalam pemilihan. Misalnya, saya tidak berpikir  --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Yes, but they're not going to bother.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, tapi mereka tidak akan repot-repot.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: People are going to use those same televised clips to make a thing.
paragraphs.cn: Orang-orang akan menggunakan klip TV yang sama untuk membuat masalah.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: And we'll use them. We'll use an audio ad overlay pictures all day long in a TV ad.
paragraphs.cn: Kami akan menggunakannya. Kami akan menggunakan audio yang ditumpangkan pada gambar sepanjang hari di iklan TV.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: Especially if they -- yes.
paragraphs.cn: Terutama jika mereka  --  --  ya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right, I want to turn to something else that's just a little bit more important, especially since we're going to have those rulings later today. There is a source telling CNN that the so-called, quote, deep state firings have begun at the Office of the Director of National Intelligence. Here's why we're watching this. President Trump's new acting spy chief, Bill Pult, he's actually in charge already, right? Not -- this hasn't been confirmed or anything like that. He's interim.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, saya ingin beralih ke hal lain yang lebih penting, terutama karena kita akan mendapatkan keputusan-keputusan itu nanti hari ini. Sumber memberi tahu CNN bahwa apa yang disebut operasi pemecatan "deep state" telah dimulai di Kantor Direktur Intelijen Nasional. Kami memperhatikan hal ini karena Pelaksana Tugas Direktur Intelijen Nasional yang baru, Bill Purtle, dari Presiden Trump, sebenarnya sudah menjabat, kan? Ini bukan  --  --  belum dikonfirmasi atau semacamnya. Dia sementara.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And Democrats are raising concerns. SEN. CHRIS VAN HOLLEN (D-MD): Bill Pulte is somebody who has zero, zero experience when it comes to national security and intelligence matters. His sort of defining mission in the Trump administration has been as a political hit man for Donald Trump to go after his political adversaries. And now this is the guy who's going to be firing people with experience and expertise when it comes to our national security intelligence matters. So, what could go wrong?
paragraphs.cn: Dan para Demokrat menyatakan kekhawatiran. Senator Chris Van Hollen (Demokrat Maryland) mengatakan: Bill Purtle tidak memiliki pengalaman dalam masalah keamanan nasional dan intelijen. Misi utamanya di pemerintahan Trump adalah bertindak sebagai preman politik Donald Trump untuk menyerang lawan politiknya. Dan sekarang, orang ini akan bertanggung jawab memecat orang-orang yang memiliki pengalaman dan keahlian dalam masalah intelijen keamanan nasional kita. Jadi, bukankah ini akan menjadi masalah?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, so here's the thing. I'm not one for the 3-D chess argument. But this is the person Trump wants. Bill Pulte, who comes from housing policy, that's not the person that anyone in the Senate seems to want.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, begini. Saya bukan tipe orang yang menganggap ini sebagai langkah tiga langkah. Tapi inilah yang diinginkan Trump. Bill Purtle berasal dari latar belakang kebijakan perumahan, yang tampaknya bukan orang yang diinginkan siapa pun di Senat.

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.text: Including Republicans.
paragraphs.cn: Termasuk Partai Republik.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Including Republicans. Let's play an example. Lindsey Graham, this Sunday on CBS, complaining that the president wasn't letting Jay Clayton, the person they actually feel like has the votes, testify. SEN. LINDSEY GRAHAM (R-SC): And I would urge President Trump to let Clayton testify. And if we nominate Clayton and get him affirmed as the new DNI, we don't want FISA to go dark. There's too many threats to our country for 702 to go down.
paragraphs.cn: Termasuk Partai Republik. Mari kita lihat contohnya. Lindsey Graham minggu ini di acara CBS mengeluh bahwa Presiden tidak mengizinkan Jay Clayton untuk bersaksi, sementara Clayton adalah orang yang mereka yakini memang memiliki suara untuk dikonfirmasi. Senator Lindsey Graham (Republik Carolina Selatan) mengatakan: Saya akan mendesak Presiden Trump untuk membiarkan Clayton bersaksi. Jika kita mencalonkan Clayton dan mengonfirmasinya sebagai Direktur Intelijen Nasional yang baru, kita tidak ingin Undang-Undang Pengawasan Intelijen Asing (FISA) berakhir. Jika Bagian 702 berakhir, terlalu banyak ancaman yang dihadapi negara kita.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Anybody who owns the shutting down of 702 under FISA will own a future attack against the United States.
paragraphs.cn: Siapa pun yang menyebabkan Bagian 702 dari FISA berakhir, akan bertanggung jawab atas serangan di masa depan terhadap Amerika Serikat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, there is this FISA surveillance law that allows the government to go after intelligence targets abroad and inadvertently maybe capture some U.S. information. The point is, his need to have Pulte looks like it's starting to hold up other aspects of the administration's agenda.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, FISA ini memungkinkan pemerintah untuk melacak target intelijen di luar negeri dan mungkin secara tidak sengaja menangkap beberapa informasi Amerika. Kuncinya adalah, dia membutuhkan Purtle, dan ini tampaknya mulai menghambat aspek lain dari agenda pemerintahan.

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.text: Yes, it is. But President Trump has been very focused for a very long time about retribution, whatever you want to call it. He feels like the intelligence community went after him in 2016. Russia, Russia, Russia. He feels like his first impeachment was the result of the intelligence community. And Bill Pulte is there to do a job.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, memang begitu. Tapi Presiden Trump sejak lama sangat fokus pada balas dendam, apa pun sebutannya. Dia merasa komunitas intelijen menargetkannya pada 2016. Rusia, Rusia, Rusia. Dia merasa pemakzulan pertamanya adalah hasil dari komunitas intelijen. Dan Bill Purtle ada di sini untuk melakukan pekerjaan itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I think what's shady to me is that --
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir yang mencurigakan bagi saya adalah  --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.text: Like to get rid of these --
paragraphs.cn: Seperti menyingkirkan orang-orang ini  --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Of course. But he's interim. Like, he has Clayton right there. He could put someone forth that Republicans are willing to give him the votes for. And instead, just to give the reviews here, here is what some of these Republicans have been saying over the last couple of weeks when Pulte's name was raised. SEN. THOM TILLIS (R-NC): I think he's the worst form of sycophant and advisor to the president that is going to hurt the president's legacy. SEN.
paragraphs.cn: Tentu. Tapi dia hanya sementara. Misalnya, dia punya Clayton di tangannya. Dia bisa mencalonkan seseorang yang bersedia dipilih oleh Partai Republik. Namun, untuk mengingatkan, berikut adalah apa yang dikatakan beberapa anggota Partai Republik ketika nama Purtle disebut dalam beberapa minggu terakhir. Senator Tom Tillis (Republik Carolina Utara) mengatakan: Saya pikir dia adalah penjilat dan penasihat presiden yang paling buruk, yang akan merusak warisan presiden. Senator

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: JOHN CORNYN (R-TX): I see no, no evidence of any qualifications for that job. SEN. KEVIN CRAMER (R-ND): I don't know Jay Clayton, but I was quite certain the acting guy wasn't going to cut it, shall we say, Pulte? SEN. JOHN KENNEDY (R-LA): What's always worried me about Mr. Pulte, he doesn't have a real deep background in intelligence.
paragraphs.cn: John Cornyn (Senator Republik Texas): Saya tidak melihat bukti bahwa dia memenuhi syarat untuk posisi itu. Kevin Cramer (Senator Republik Dakota Utara): Saya tidak kenal Jay Clayton, tapi saya cukup yakin orang yang menjabat sementara itu tidak bisa melakukan pekerjaan itu, begitulah, Purtle? John Kennedy (Senator Republik Louisiana): Yang selalu saya khawatirkan tentang Tn. Purtle adalah bahwa dia tidak memiliki latar belakang yang benar-benar mendalam di bidang intelijen.

paragraphs.speaker: Manu Raju
paragraphs.text: And Pulte, Pulte's (INAUDIBLE).
paragraphs.cn: Dan Purtle, Purtle (tidak jelas).

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I have no observations on the matter.
paragraphs.cn: Saya tidak punya pendapat tentang masalah ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I'm going to start using that one, actually. No, keep in mind, this person --
paragraphs.cn: I actually plan to start using that phrase. No, remember, this person -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Actually, they (ph) just laugh after he said that. Did you see that?
paragraphs.cn: Actually, after he said that, they (slip of the tongue) laughed. Did you see that?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That the person they're describing is now already firing people.
paragraphs.cn: The person they described is now firing people.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Well, I'm OK with actually -- like taking everything else out of it. There's so many things here. First of all, I'm hoping that Clayton issue gets solved tomorrow when the president goes up and talks to Senate Republicans because I actually think that is part of a broader issue. Pulte is in there for one thing, as everyone said, which is to get rid of a lot of these people. I was around when we created this agency, the DNI.
paragraphs.cn: Well, I can actually accept -- -- like setting everything else aside. There's a lot going on here. First, I hope Clayton's issue gets resolved tomorrow when the president talks to Senate Republicans, because I actually think this is part of a broader problem. Pult is doing one thing there, as everyone says, which is getting rid of a lot of people. I was there when we created this agency -- -- the Office of the Director of National Intelligence.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: I think that there's a lot of issues with it, and there's a lot of fat, but there's also a lot of leaks. The problem is, is Bill is a little bit -- not a little bit. Bill is a lightning rod because he doesn't have any internet (ph). He doesn't have any background in intelligence.
paragraphs.cn: I think it has a lot of problems, a lot of redundancy, but also a lot of leaks. The issue is, Bill is a bit -- -- not a bit. Bill is a lightning rod because he has no network (slip of the tongue). He has no intelligence background.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Is that the only reason it's a lightning rod, Ashley?
paragraphs.cn: Is that the only reason he's a lightning rod, Ashley?

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Well, no. He's also a lightning rod just because of all of his policies. I mean he actually did a lot of cage rattling within the -- in the West Wing. So, there is -- there's not a big -- there's not a -- he has a lot of enemies and a lot of allies, but his number one -- the number --
paragraphs.cn: Well, no. He's also a lightning rod just because of all his policies. I mean, he's caused a lot of upheaval inside the West Wing. So, he has many enemies and many allies, but his primary -- -- primary -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: Didn't somebody already hit him at the White House? Didn't somebody punch him in the face?
paragraphs.cn: Hasn't someone already punched him in the White House? Didn't anyone punch him in the face?

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: No, that wasn't -- that was -- that was Secretary Bessent.
paragraphs.cn: No, that wasn't -- -- that was -- -- that was Secretary Bessent.

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.text: And he just threatened to.
paragraphs.cn: And he only threatened to do it.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, just threatened. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: Right, just threatened. Yes.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: I get my MMA mixed up all the time at the White House.
paragraphs.cn: I always get mixed up with MMA in the White House.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: But just -- I just want to say something, like, yes, that's true.
paragraphs.cn: But what I'm trying to say is, yes, that's true.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: There is something that's really important. The DNI agency is the most leaked agency in the intelligence world. There needs to be changes there. It's just, the controversy is around the person that's doing it.
paragraphs.cn: One thing is very important. The Office of the Director of National Intelligence is the most leaky agency in the intelligence community. That needs to change. The controversy is just about the person doing it.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Should a person who doesn't have the job yet, is not confirmed by the Senate, be firing people already? That seems like it opens the door for people --
paragraphs.cn: Should someone who hasn't taken office yet, not confirmed by the Senate, start firing people? That seems to open the door for -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: If they're leaking stuff, I don't care who fires them.
paragraphs.cn: If they're leaking stuff, I don't care who fires them.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But we don't know why. That's the thing.
paragraphs.cn: But we don't know the reason. That's the problem.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Well, there's major investigations going on that are classified of who's leaking. So, yes, I think that if -- those people should be fired if they're leaking national --
paragraphs.cn: Well, there are some major classified investigations going on about who is leaking. So, yes, I think if -- -- those who leak national secrets should be fired.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: I think Trump's going to get exactly what he wants. He wants this.
paragraphs.cn: I think Trump will get what he wants. He wants this.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: Trump wants Pulte firing everybody. And then he wants to say, OK, we got rid of that bad guy. We'll bring Clayton in. Everybody likes this Clayton guy because they put up the worst of the worst of somebody that's totally not qualified to do this job.
