paragraphs.cn: Trump wants Pult to fire everyone. Then he wants to say, okay, we got rid of that bad guy. We'll bring in Clayton. Everyone likes this Clayton because they put forward the worst, most unqualified person for the job.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It seems like the recipe for a lawsuit. But, like, I'm not a lawyer. I don't know.
paragraphs.cn: This seems like it would invite lawsuits. But, I'm not a lawyer. I don't know.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: But I think -- well, I'm -- I mean, that's like the everyone is --
paragraphs.cn: But I think -- -- well, I mean, it's like everyone is -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Do you know what I mean? Like, it kind of feels like, are you fired without cause? Like, can you raise the question? (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: You know what I mean? It feels like, were you fired without cause? Can you raise that question? (cross talk)

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: I like to -- I'm making my prediction. I think Clayton's confirmed in July.
paragraphs.cn: I think -- -- I'm making a prediction. I think Clayton will be confirmed in July.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I think he's -- you're both right. He's confirmed. But after a bunch of firings.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir dia -- -- kalian berdua benar. Dia akan dikonfirmasi. Tapi setelah serangkaian pemecatan.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: No, in July. I think he gets confirmed in July.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, pada bulan Juli. Saya pikir dia akan dikonfirmasi pada bulan Juli.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: (INAUDIBLE). Tell her (INAUDIBLE). Here we go.
paragraphs.cn: (Tidak terdengar). Katakan padanya (tidak terdengar). Ayo mulai.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, you guys, there's one more. I want to talk about this. This is actually really interesting. Next on CNN THIS MORNING, Tucker Carlson claiming he's out of the Republican Party. This is years after defending Trump to the MAGA movement. So, we're wondering where he's going to take his support next. And then later on CNN, a White House plot grows. There are new arrests revealing drones, explosives and a widening network connected with the alleged plot to attack the UFC event.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, semuanya, satu hal lagi. Saya ingin membahas ini. Ini sebenarnya sangat menarik. Selanjutnya di CNN This Morning, Tucker Carlson mengklaim dia keluar dari Partai Republik. Ini terjadi setelah bertahun-tahun dia membela Trump dalam gerakan MAGA. Jadi, kami ingin tahu ke mana dia akan mengalihkan dukungannya. Kemudian di CNN, sebuah konspirasi Gedung Putih mulai terungkap. Penangkapan baru mengungkap drone, bahan peledak, dan jaringan yang terus berkembang terkait dengan dugaan rencana penyerangan di acara UFC.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, the Democratic Party, as we talked about, continues to struggle with its messaging on Israel in the wake of the '24 election. Today, as voters in New York head to the polls, a pro-Israeli lobbying group highlights a rift in the party. AIPAC, American Israel Public Affairs Committee, it's donated nearly $42 million to campaigns this year alone. And several high-profile Democrats, Cory Booker, Gavin Newsom, AOC, say they refuse to accept AIPAC funds.
paragraphs.cn: Seperti yang kita bahas sebelumnya, Partai Demokrat masih berjuang dengan pesan mereka tentang Israel setelah pemilu 2024. Hari ini, saat pemilih New York pergi ke tempat pemungutan suara, sebuah kelompok lobi pro-Israel menyoroti perpecahan di dalam partai. AIPAC, atau American Israel Public Affairs Committee, telah menyumbang hampir $42 juta untuk kampanye tahun ini saja. Dan beberapa tokoh Demokrat terkenal, Cory Booker, Gavin Newsom, AOC, mengatakan mereka menolak dana AIPAC.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: In New York, Mayor Zohran Mamdani also calling out candidates who do not take that money -- candidates who do take that money. MAYOR ZOHRAN MAMDANI (D),
paragraphs.cn: Di New York, Wali Kota Zoran Mamdani juga mengecam kandidat yang menerima dana tersebut -- -- dan mereka yang tidak menerimanya. Wali Kota Zoran Mamdani (Demokrat):

paragraphs.speaker: New York City
paragraphs.text: The old world is dying and the new world struggles to be born. Now is the time of monsters. These monsters take many forms today. In AIPAC, for whom the only thing more frightening than democracy being allowed to run its course is an end to genocide in Netanyahu's wars. They move millions in dark money to accomplish a single goal, to preserve their power so that they can turn us against one another, instead of our leaders turning towards the moral change we all know to be necessary.
paragraphs.cn: Dunia lama sedang sekarat, dunia baru berjuang untuk lahir. Ini adalah zaman monster. Monster-monster ini muncul dalam berbagai bentuk hari ini. Di AIPAC, satu-satunya hal yang lebih menakutkan daripada membiarkan demokrasi berkembang secara alami adalah mengakhiri genosida dalam perang Netanyahu. Mereka menggunakan jutaan uang hitam untuk mencapai satu tujuan: mempertahankan kekuasaan mereka sehingga mereka bisa membuat kita saling bertentangan, daripada membuat para pemimpin kita beralih ke perubahan moral yang kita semua tahu diperlukan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. He was specifically pointing to Congressman Adriano Espaillat, who is running against one of Mamdani's backed candidates. Now, according to the Federal Election Commission, AIPAC made two donations last month totaling around $600,000. Ted Deutch, the CEO of the American Jewish Committee, called Mamdani's comments, quote, "outrageous and dangerous, and the impact of your words extend far beyond politics." Mamdani has doubled down. MAYOR ZOHRAN MAMDANI (D),
paragraphs.cn: Baik. Dia secara khusus menunjuk Anggota Kongres Adriano Espaillat, yang bersaing dengan kandidat yang didukung Mamdani. Menurut data Federal Election Commission, AIPAC melakukan dua donasi bulan lalu dengan total sekitar $600.000. CEO American Jewish Committee Ted Deutch menyebut pernyataan Mamdani "sangat keterlaluan dan berbahaya, dampak kata-kata Anda jauh melampaui politik." Mamdani bersikukuh. Wali Kota Zoran Mamdani (Demokrat):

paragraphs.speaker: New York
paragraphs.text: I want to be very clear. We're talking about a status quo where children are being killed on a daily basis. And when I am speaking about AIPAC, I'm speaking about an organization that has been supportive of the status quo, that has fought any attempt to actually deliver safety to people, not just in Palestine, but, frankly, through much of the region. And it is a status quo for immorality. It is one that I will not accept.
paragraphs.cn: Saya ingin mengatakan dengan jelas. Yang kita bicarakan adalah situasi di mana anak-anak dibunuh setiap hari. Ketika saya berbicara tentang AIPAC, saya merujuk pada organisasi yang mendukung status quo ini, yang menentang setiap upaya nyata untuk membawa keamanan bagi rakyat, tidak hanya di Palestina, tetapi terus terang, di sebagian besar wilayah. Ini adalah status quo yang tidak bermoral. Saya tidak akan menerimanya.

paragraphs.speaker: New York
paragraphs.text: And I think that it is important that when we ask ourselves how such death and destruction is happening overseas, we also name those who allow it to take place.
paragraphs.cn: Dan saya pikir, ketika kita bertanya pada diri sendiri mengapa begitu banyak kematian dan kehancuran terjadi di luar negeri, kita juga harus menyebut nama mereka yang membiarkan semua ini terjadi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Joining me now, Errol Louis, political anchor with Spectrum News. Errol, in a minute I want to play for you Rep, Dan Goldman, who had kind of an altercation with a coffee shop around this issue of anti- Semitism. But first, can you talk about how this AIPAC conversation, why it's playing such a big role in these essentially small city races?
paragraphs.cn: Bergabung dengan saya sekarang adalah Errol Louis, pembawa acara politik Spectrum News. Errol, nanti saya ingin memutar klip Anggota Kongres Dan Goldman untuk Anda, yang terlibat pertengkaran dengan sebuah kedai kopi tentang isu antisemitisme. Tapi pertama, bisakah Anda berbicara tentang percakapan seputar AIPAC ini, mengapa ini memainkan peran penting dalam pemilihan yang pada dasarnya adalah kota kecil ini?

paragraphs.speaker: Errol Louis
paragraphs.text: Yes. Good morning, Audie. The issue of AIPAC is really shorthand for opposing Israel's policies in Gaza and elsewhere in the region. And so, they've done what politicians do with slogans. They've made it so that it basically ends the conversation just to say, hey, you took money from AIPAC and then you can't say much more about it. And that's, you know, this is -- this is what happens in the middle of a heated campaign.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Selamat pagi, Audi. Masalah AIPAC sebenarnya adalah singkatan dari oposisi terhadap kebijakan Israel di Gaza dan bagian lain di kawasan ini. Jadi, mereka melakukan apa yang dilakukan politisi dengan slogan. Mereka membuat kalimat ini pada dasarnya mengakhiri percakapan: Hei, Anda menerima uang dari AIPAC, lalu Anda tidak bisa berkata apa-apa lagi. Anda tahu, itu -- -- itulah yang terjadi selama kampanye yang sengit.

paragraphs.speaker: Errol Louis
paragraphs.text: It's a little unusual because just as the mayor talks about this and has made it really central to a lot of his politics and a lot of the politics of these congressional races, there are a ton of other issues. I mean we're always talking about affordability, and somehow this issue has taken what some would call an outsized level of influence when it comes to all of these local races.
paragraphs.cn: Ini agak tidak biasa, karena seperti yang dibicarakan wali kota dan menjadikannya inti dari banyak aktivitas politiknya dan politik dalam pemilihan kongres ini, masih banyak masalah lain. Maksud saya, kita selalu berbicara tentang keterjangkauan, tetapi entah bagaimana, masalah ini memiliki pengaruh yang berlebihan, menurut beberapa orang, dalam semua pemilihan lokal ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Is it also because culturally what's happened on the streets, even since Mamdani's election, right, since October 7th, but then since Mamdani's election itself, when you had people saying, oh, we're going to leave the city if he wins, right, raising over these concerns. In particular, I want to flag for you something that happened downtown, I think it was Brooklyn. I'm not sure.
paragraphs.cn: Apakah juga karena secara budaya apa yang terjadi di jalanan, bahkan sejak Mamdani terpilih, kan, sejak 7 Oktober, tetapi sejak Mamdani terpilih sendiri, ketika ada yang berkata, oh, jika dia menang kami akan meninggalkan kota ini, kan, mengangkat kekhawatiran ini. Secara khusus, saya ingin mengingatkan Anda tentang sesuatu yang terjadi di pusat kota, saya pikir di Brooklyn. Saya tidak terlalu yakin.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And you've got this guy, Congressman Goldman. And he's dealing with a bit of a firestorm. He was visiting a coffee shop there. And after he visited, they posted that he was banned over his support for Israel. And they wrote, "we see that you stopped by our shop for coffee. Do you see how it doesn't taste like genocide juice? Or are you still having a hard time telling the difference?" They go on to say, "we don't serve racist, fascists, homophobes, genocide enablers, or anyone in between.
paragraphs.cn: Dan juga Anggota Kongres Goldman ini. Dia menghadapi kontroversi. Dia pergi ke sebuah kedai kopi. Setelah dia pergi, pemilik toko memposting bahwa dia dilarang masuk karena dukungannya terhadap Israel. Mereka menulis: "Kami melihat Anda mampir ke toko kami untuk minum kopi. Apakah Anda merasa kopi ini tidak terasa seperti jus genosida? Atau Anda masih tidak bisa membedakannya?" Mereka melanjutkan: "Kami tidak melayani rasis, fasis, homofobik, kaki tangan genosida, atau siapa pun di antaranya."

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Too bad we didn't recognize you right away." So, Goldman responded. And here's what he had to say. REP. DAN GOLDMAN (D-NY): And I think it's really dangerous when people start conflating the actions of the Israeli government with American Jews who have absolutely nothing to do with what is going on in Israel and the rise in anti-Semitism, because of what is happening in the Middle East, here in the United States, is really dangerous and it is escalating significantly.
paragraphs.cn: "Sayangnya kami tidak segera mengenali Anda." Goldman menanggapi hal ini. Berikut pernyataannya. Anggota Kongres Dan Goldman (D-NY): Saya pikir sangat berbahaya ketika orang mulai menyamakan tindakan pemerintah Israel dengan orang Yahudi Amerika, yang sama sekali tidak ada hubungannya dengan apa yang terjadi di Israel, dan karena apa yang terjadi di Timur Tengah, peningkatan antisemitisme di Amerika sangat berbahaya dan meningkat secara signifikan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Errol, I don't know if you had heard about this incident --
paragraphs.cn: Errol, saya tidak tahu apakah Anda mendengar tentang ini -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But what do you make of how he is, you know, articulating this?
paragraphs.cn: Tapi bagaimana Anda melihat cara dia menjelaskan hal ini?

paragraphs.speaker: Louis
paragraphs.text: Yes, I did hear about the incident. It's not far from where I'm sitting right now, in fact, in Brooklyn. And very disturbing. Very disturbing. This was -- he went in because he needed to use the bathroom. He was with his seven-year-old daughter while he was out campaigning. And they were nice enough to let him do it. And he bought a cup of coffee. And then after the fact, they published this photo and that statement and that kind of a thing. Listen, the issues in the Middle East are so complicated.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, saya memang mendengar tentang ini. Faktanya, tidak jauh dari tempat saya duduk sekarang, di Brooklyn. Sangat meresahkan. Sangat meresahkan. Saat itu -- -- dia masuk karena perlu ke kamar kecil. Dia sedang berkampanye dengan putrinya yang berusia tujuh tahun. Mereka dengan baik hati mengizinkannya menggunakannya. Dia juga membeli secangkir kopi. Setelah itu, mereka memposting foto itu dan pernyataan itu dan sebagainya. Dengar, masalah Timur Tengah sangat kompleks.

paragraphs.speaker: Louis
paragraphs.text: Issues like genocide, fascism, you know, these buzzwords that some of these activists like to throw around with such abandon are the opposite of trying to find some kind of a path forward and some kind of a civil discourse, which is hopefully what you'll have, including during a political campaign of this kind. It's a real -- really a lost opportunity. But make no mistake about it, this is a group of activists who have declared war on the mainstream Democratic Party.
paragraphs.cn: Masalah seperti genosida, fasisme, Anda tahu, itu adalah kata-kata populer yang suka dilontarkan oleh beberapa aktivis, yang justru bertentangan dengan upaya menemukan jalan ke depan dan dialog yang beradab, yang diinginkan orang, termasuk selama masa kampanye politik ini. Ini benar-benar kesempatan yang terlewatkan. Tapi jangan salah, ini adalah sekelompok aktivis yang menyatakan perang terhadap Demokrat arus utama.

paragraphs.speaker: Louis
paragraphs.text: And they are using these buzzwords, they are using these issues, that kind of rough style where they try and target people and personalize it is what they're trying to bring to politics. And they say that this is going to be the path forward. We'll see later today, tonight, whether or not New York voters are going to go for that.
paragraphs.cn: Mereka menggunakan kata-kata populer ini, memanfaatkan masalah ini, gaya kasar itu, mencoba menargetkan individu dan membuatnya pribadi, itulah yang mereka coba bawa ke dalam politik. Mereka mengatakan ini adalah jalan ke depan. Kita akan melihat nanti hari ini, malam ini apakah pemilih New York akan menerima ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That's Errol Louis. As always, totally appreciate your time. Thank you. So, we've been talking about this issue, whether this could affect the fate of these politicians in New York. And it appears Israel sealed the fate of Tucker Carlson as well. For months, Republicans have been painting him as anti-Semitic. SEN. TED CRUZ (R-TX): And I will say on the right, I have seen more anti-Semitism in the last 18 months on the right. It is being spread by loud voices, the most consequential of whom is Tucker Carlson.
paragraphs.cn: Ini Errol Louis. Seperti biasa, terima kasih banyak atas waktu Anda. Terima kasih. Jadi, kita telah membahas masalah ini, apakah ini akan mempengaruhi nasib para politisi New York ini. Tampaknya Israel juga menentukan nasib Tucker Carlson. Selama berbulan-bulan, anggota Partai Republik menyebutnya antisemit. Senator Ted Cruz (R-TX): Saya akan mengatakan, di sayap kanan, saya melihat lebih banyak antisemitisme dalam 18 bulan terakhir. Itu disebarkan oleh beberapa suara keras, yang paling penting adalah Tucker Carlson.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I believe Tucker Carlson is the single most dangerous demagogue in this country.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir Tucker Carlson adalah penghasut paling berbahaya di negara ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish

paragraphs.speaker: Tucker Carlson

paragraphs.cn: Saya tidak akan mendukung Partai Republik. Saya sama sekali tidak mungkin mendukung Partai Republik. Saya juga tidak akan mendukung Partai Demokrat. paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.text: I think that what is happening here with Tucker Carlson, with Ted Cruz is various parts of what has been the Republican coalition figuring out what happens in a potential post-Trump reality and positioning themselves.
